claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I can give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To start, I need to ask you a few questions.

Do you have any children for whom you'd like to claim Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child - Alex, who's 17.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in college doing A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living situation:

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester - and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

Are you or your partner (if you have one) earning over £60,000 per year? This won't affect your eligibility, but it may mean you need to pay a tax charge.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided, let me give you my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why you qualify:
- Although Alex is 17 (over the usual age 16 limit), Child Benefit continues for young people under 20 who are in approved education or training
- Alex's A-levels at college count as approved education
- You live with Alex in the UK and are responsible for them
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, you won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge either.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If Alex's circumstances change (such as leaving education), you'll need to notify HMRC as this could affect your entitlement.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but you'll also qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. You are responsible for Alex as you live together in the UK (Manchester), and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives with their 17-year-old child Alex in Manchester, UK. Alex is in approved education (A-levels at college), which extends Child Benefit eligibility beyond age 16 up to age 20. No one else is claiming for Alex, and the claimant is responsible for the child. All eligibility criteria are met. Additionally, neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, so no High Income Child Benefit tax charge would apply.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college). According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but you'll also qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. You are responsible for Alex as you live together in the UK (Manchester), and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives with their 17-year-old child Alex in Manchester, UK. Alex is in approved education (A-levels at college), which extends Child Benefit eligibility beyond age 16 up to age 20. No one else is claiming for Alex, and the claimant is responsible for the child. All eligibility criteria are met. Additionally, neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, so no High Income Child Benefit tax charge would apply.
